Dorms described as functional but unremarkable by current students
Move-in day is chaotic with all freshmen arriving simultaneously

key_points: Small school means limited social scene compared to large universities
Athletics exist but school is not a major sports culture campus
March Madness run generated rare burst of school pride and buzz

key_points: No dedicated mental health support feedback found in sources
Students mention stress around cost and financial uncertainty
Visa rejections and deferment denials create significant anxiety for international students

key_points: Scholarships available and can be substantial — one student got near full ride
Private school tuition is high; students actively seek ways to cut costs
AACSB accreditation mentioned as justification for cost at Metro campus

The school also gave me the most scholarships

key_points: Professors are approachable and know students by name — small class advantage
AACSB-accredited business school is a legitimate credential worth noting
CS and IT programs exist but peers often recommend NJIT over FDU for tech
key_quotes: The professors seem very knowledgeable about the courses. They are approachable, and they also know my name.

Mandatory English placement test (EPT) surprises incoming students after offer letters
International student communication appears inconsistent and stressful

Metro campus proximity to NYC is a genuine career and lifestyle asset
Vancouver campus serves a heavily international South Asian student population
